name: cc-sdd
description: Kiro-style spec-driven development toolkit with 17 skills (requirements → design → tasks → implementation) that installs into Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and Antigravity.

description.value: Kiro-style spec-driven development toolkit that installs 17 skills enforcing a requirements → design → tasks → implementation workflow across eight AI coding agents.

website.comment: No dedicated marketing site; the GitHub repo serves as the landing page.

first-release.comment: Earliest tagged release v2.0.1 on 2025-11-10; the project may have predated this tag (numbering starts at 2.x), but no earlier public releases are listed.

covered-phases.comment: Skills map to phases: /kiro-discovery (discovery → brief.md), /kiro-spec-init + /kiro-spec-requirements (spec), /kiro-spec-design (architecture, with Mermaid diagrams), /kiro-spec-tasks (planning/tasks), /kiro-impl (implementation with per-task TDD = testing, plus an independent reviewer = review).

methodology-style.comment: Self-described as Kiro-style spec-driven; spec is the central artifact (spec-centric); /kiro-impl performs per-task TDD (RED → GREEN) as a hard rule (strict-tdd); ships as a slash-command/skill harness installed into the agent of choice (harness-centric).

opinionated.comment: Enforces a fixed requirements → design → tasks → implementation pipeline with per-phase artifacts; deviation requires bypassing skills.

ceremony-level.comment: Multiple distinct artifacts (brief, requirements, design, tasks), explicit phase gates with human approval, mandatory TDD and reviewer pass per task.

mechanization-level.comment: Single npx command installs the workflow; phases run via dedicated slash commands inside the agent. Below very-high because there is no managed end-to-end pipeline outside the host agent.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Documented as 'humans approve the contract at phase gates' between requirements, design, and tasks.

approval-gates.comment: Approval gates between requirements (spec), design (plan/architecture), and tasks; implementation runs autonomously after the tasks contract is approved.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Implementation phase (/kiro-impl) is autonomous per task, but the upstream phase gates expect human approval; not configured for true idea-to-merge autonomy by default.

autonomy-level.comment: Phase-level approvals (spec/design/tasks); within an approved task /kiro-impl is autonomous including TDD loop and auto-debug, matching the rubric's '3 = phase-level approvals'.

rollback-support.comment: No built-in revert mechanism documented; TDD and per-task scoping reduce blast radius but do not provide undo.

supported-agents.comment: Eight first-party targets: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and Antigravity. Gemini CLI and Antigravity tagged as 'other' because they have no dedicated tag in attributes.json.

editor-integrations.comment: The skills run wherever the host agent runs: VS Code (Copilot, Cursor as a VS Code fork), and terminal CLIs (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI). Windsurf and Antigravity are standalone editors not represented by a dedicated tag.

model-agnostic.comment: Skills are prompts/markdown executed by whichever model the host agent is configured to use; no model is hardcoded.

mcp-support.comment: No mention of Model Context Protocol in the README, package, or release notes as of 2026-04-21.

artifact-formats.comment: All produced artifacts (brief.md, roadmap.md, requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md) are markdown.

artifacts-covered.comment: brief.md (discovery), roadmap.md (multi-spec initiatives), requirements.md (EARS-format specs), design.md (architecture with Mermaid + file structure plan), tasks.md (plans/tasks with dependencies).

project-layout.value: .kiro/specs/, .kiro/steering/, and .kiro/settings/ shared across agents; skills land in per-agent dirs such as .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/ (Codex), .cursor/skills/, .github/skills/ (Copilot), .windsurf/skills/, .opencode/skills/, .gemini/skills/, and .agent/skills/ (Antigravity); also writes a top-level CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md per agent.

artifacts-in-git.comment: All artifacts and steering files are written into the repo tree under .kiro/ and committed alongside source.

language-agnostic.comment: Workflow is prompt/markdown driven and ships no language-specific tooling; works for any stack the host agent can edit.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Skills enforce process via prompt instructions (instructional); /kiro-impl mandates per-task TDD with RED → GREEN (test-gates); each task is verified by an independent reviewer agent (agent-review); phase contracts require human sign-off (manual-approval). No CI integration shipped by default.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Kiro-style spec → design → tasks → implementation is enforced; each later skill expects the upstream artifact to exist.

artifact-validation.comment: Artifacts use templates and conventions (e.g., EARS-format requirements) but no schema validator is shipped; /kiro-validate-gap exists for content gap-checks but is optional and prompt-based.

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent context lives in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md and the .kiro/steering/ markdown files maintained by /kiro-steering.

persistent-memory.comment: Steering files and the .kiro/specs/ tree persist between sessions and are shared by every command.

project-context-files.comment: Generates a CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md alongside .kiro/steering/ guidelines.

cost-tracking.comment: No token/cost tracking is shipped by cc-sdd itself; tracking is delegated to the host agent.

run-history.comment: No structured run history beyond the artifacts the workflow writes to disk.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collection is documented; the package is a local file generator with no reporting endpoint, so users are effectively opted out by default.

custom-templates.comment: Templates and rules are user-editable under .kiro/settings/templates/ and .kiro/settings/rules/.

sub-agents.comment: Skills themselves are user-editable, and /kiro-impl spawns role-specific sub-agents (fresh implementer, independent reviewer, auto-debug) that users can customize via the rules/templates.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or shared template directory exists; customization is per-project.

install-mechanism.comment: Distributed as the cc-sdd npm package, run via `npx cc-sdd@latest`.

setup-time.comment: Single `npx cc-sdd@latest` invocation produces a working skill set per chosen agent.

self-hosted.comment: Pure local file generator; no hosted service involved.

requires-account.comment: No vendor account required for cc-sdd itself; the host agent may require its own.

git-worktree-support.comment: No documented worktree integration; per-task batching does not branch automatically.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive Skill Reference, Command Reference, and Customization Guide; READMEs in 13 languages. Held back from 5 by lack of dedicated tutorial walkthroughs and case studies.

active-maintenance.comment: v3.0.2 released 2026-04-13, eight days before research; pushed_at 2026-04-14.

example-projects.comment: No example repositories or documented case studies of real-world use found in the README or docs.

community-channel.comment: No Discord, Slack, forum, or GitHub Discussions presence advertised; community engagement appears limited to GitHub issues and Japanese Zenn articles.
